title: Diphyes verruculata
taxonomy: Diphyes verruculata is classified as a species of sea cucumber, belonging to the class Holothuroidea. This species is part of the family Elpidiidae, which includes various species of deep-sea sea cucumbers. Diphyes verruculata is a marine invertebrate, and its classification is also reflected in the phylum Echinodermata, which includes starfish, sea urchins, and other marine animals. The species is further categorized into the order Elasipodida and the genus Diphyes. The classification of Diphyes verruculata highlights its unique position within the echinoderm family.

side_effects: Diphyes verruculata, a plant species found in Australia, has been reported to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals.
